section1.heading: 1. General Provisions
section1.paragraph1: This Privacy Policy explains how SotsiaalAI OÜ processes personal data when you use the SotsiaalAI web platform. It covers account creation, sign-in, subscriptions, chats, rooms, help requests and offers, documents, deep research, file uploads, logs, and other platform workflows. It also explains what data is stored, for how long, how feature limits and storage quotas can affect retention, and which automated systems are used to generate responses, drafts, classifications, or search results.
section1.paragraph2: SotsiaalAI processes personal data in accordance with the Estonian Personal Data Protection Act and the EU General Data Protection Regulation. If you have questions about data processing, contact us before continuing to use the service.

section2.heading: 2. Data We Collect
section2.paragraph1: 2.1 Account and authentication data. We process your email address, user role, password/PIN hash, email verification state, temporary sign-in tokens, one-time code records, trusted-device data, and any profile data you choose to add or update.
section2.paragraph2: 2.2 Service usage data. We process conversations and conversation messages, room messages and membership data, help requests, help offers, help matches, research requests and job metadata, document uploads, temporary chat files, agent drafts and final outputs, invites, subscription and sponsored-access status, payment metadata, audit records, and storage- or quota-related metadata needed to run those workflows.
section2.paragraph3: 2.3 Technical and local data. We process IP address, browser and device information, usage, rate-limit, and error logs, security events, and technical events related to AI, search, and speech features. The platform also uses essential cookies and browser storage to keep language, accessibility settings, active conversation state, PIN keypad preferences, and other usability settings.

section3.heading: 3. Purposes and Legal Bases
section3.items:
• Account creation, sign-in, role handling, subscription management, and core platform features are processed for the performance of a contract (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)).
  • Payments, fraud prevention, security checks, logging, and service reliability are processed for the performance of a contract and for legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(b),(f)).
  • Compliance with legal obligations, accounting, and claim defense are processed on the basis of legal obligation and legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(c),(f)).
  • Microphone access, browser speech, and device-local storage are used only when the user triggers or allows the relevant feature.
  • Help listings, rooms, and invite flows are processed to operate the service and to mediate communication initiated by the user.

section4.heading: 4. How Platform Content Is Used
section4.body:
4.1 Chats and rooms. Chat and room messages are used to generate responses, show history, and enable collaboration. A conversation can also contain workflow state, such as whether it is locked into document drafting, a help request, a help offer flow, or a deep research task.

4.2 Help requests, help offers, and matching. When you save a help request or help offer, it becomes visible to other users in the relevant listing views according to the platform workflow. When you connect a request and an offer, the platform creates or reopens a room and shares the necessary details with the involved parties.

4.3 Documents and agent outputs. Files uploaded to Documents and the drafts or final outputs created from them are stored in the user's workspace until deleted by the user, removed with the account, or cleaned up under retention or storage-quota rules. They are not made public to other users unless the user explicitly shares them through platform actions.

4.4 Temporary chat files. Files added in the chat document workflow or analysis flow are meant only for that specific conversation or session. They are not automatically added to the persistent Documents library unless the user separately uses the document-library workflow.

4.5 Voice features. Dictation audio is processed only to create text. Read-aloud uses chat text to produce audio playback. These features are not used for marketing or user profiling.

4.6 Automated processing. Some workflows use automated systems to route conversations, classify requests, retrieve documents, rank results, and generate responses or drafts. These systems support the service but do not replace the user's own judgment or create solely automated decisions with legal or similarly significant effects.

4.8 Privacy pre-checks. In sensitive workflows, the platform may use local or server-side pre-checks that detect simple signals of personal data, contact details, personal identification numbers, addresses, child-safety concerns, or crisis risk. This helps warn the user and minimise data, but it is not full anonymisation or a legal compliance certification.

4.9 Role-based access. More sensitive capabilities are made available based on role, organisation, professional-use confirmation, and technical permissions. An administrator or organisation role does not by default grant access to a user's private conversations, documents, or files.

section5.heading: 5. Processors
section5.body: SotsiaalAI uses processors where needed to provide the service, for example payment processing, email delivery, AI and speech services, hosting, and logging infrastructure. Processors handle data only under SotsiaalAI's instructions and subject to contractual and technical safeguards.

section6.heading: 6. Data Disclosure and Sharing
section6.body:
SotsiaalAI does not sell personal data. Data is shared only to the extent needed to run the service: with processors, with the payment provider, with users you invite or are matched with in rooms and help-mediation workflows, and with competent authorities where required by law.

A user's personal conversations and documents are linked to that user's account. Room and group conversation content is visible to room members, and only to the extent needed for the relevant workflow. An organisation or employer administrator is not automatically given general access to an employee's personal conversations, documents, or files.

Platform administrator access to content is exceptional technical administration access used only where justified, for example to provide support, investigate an error or security incident, maintain service reliability, prevent abuse, or comply with a legal claim or obligation.

If you create an invite, save a visible help listing, or connect parties through a room, the relevant information is shared inside the platform only with the users for whom that workflow is intended.

If a user or organisation representative accepts a professional-use or data-processing framework, evidence records related to that acceptance may be retained and, where necessary, shared with the relevant organisation or user to prove the existence, version, and time of acceptance.

section7.heading: 7. Retention
section7.body:
7.1 Account and profile data. Account and profile data is generally retained until the account is deleted, except where the law requires longer retention.

7.2 Temporary authentication data. An email verification link is generally retained for up to 24 hours, a PIN reset token for up to 60 minutes, a one-time sign-in code for up to 15 minutes, a temporary sign-in token for up to 15 minutes, and a trusted-device record for up to 30 days. Expired authentication records are removed regularly.

7.3 Conversations and current work data. Conversations, chat messages, current conversation state records, and general usage logs are generally retained for up to 90 days from the last activity or from the relevant record's creation or update time.

7.4 Rooms and help data. Inactive rooms and related messages are generally retained for up to 90 days after the last substantive activity. Closed, cancelled, or archived help requests and help offers, and completed or rejected matches, are generally retained for up to 90 days after the last update.

7.5 Documents and agent outputs. Files uploaded to the document library, templates, document drafting workspace drafts, and confirmed agent outputs are generally retained for up to 90 days from the last modification. If an older document is technically linked to a newer output, it may be retained briefly until the newer link ends.

7.6 Deletion and deletion-job tracking. When a user deletes a document, it is removed from active document management and deletion of the related file and RAG or search-index reference is triggered where such a reference exists. If deletion from an external index does not succeed immediately, a technical deletion-job record may remain so that a failed or pending deletion is visible and can be followed up.

7.7 Account deletion. When an account is deleted, the user account and related active user data are removed according to the application's deletion logic. Technical audit records and deletion-job records may remain as limited metadata to prove the deletion action, handle pending deletion jobs, maintain security, or comply with legal obligations.

7.8 Backups and operational logs. If content is deleted by the user, the account is deleted, or a record is removed because of quota or cleanup rules, it may briefly remain in backups, system or proxy logs, or service-provider technical logs for recovery, security, troubleshooting, or legal-compliance purposes, and is then removed or anonymised according to the relevant operations or provider configuration.

7.9 Audits, acceptances, and payments. General document audit records, technical processing logs, and evidence records related to professional-use or data-processing frameworks are generally retained for up to 90 days, unless a longer period is reasonably needed to prove a specific acceptance, contractual relationship, or legal obligation. A detailed raw response or payload received from the payment provider is generally truncated or removed after 90 days. The minimal payment and accounting record is retained for up to 7 years or longer if required by applicable law.

section8.heading: 8. Your Rights
section8.items:
• Right to access your personal data and obtain a copy.
  • Right to correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
  • Right to request erasure where there is a legal basis for it.
  • Right to restrict processing or object to processing based on legitimate interest.
  • Right to data portability, where the GDPR conditions for portability are met.
  • Right to withdraw consent, where processing is based on consent.
  • Right to request human review where automated processing or AI output requires it.
  • Right to lodge a complaint with the Data Protection Inspectorate.

section9.heading: 9. Cookies and Browser Storage
section9.body: SotsiaalAI uses essential cookies and browser storage for sign-in, session handling, language, accessibility, trusted-device state, active conversation state, and other usability preferences. We do not use advertising cookies or third-party marketing trackers. The public product UI also does not currently use advertising trackers.

section10.heading: 10. Security
section10.body:
We apply risk-based technical and organisational safeguards, including encrypted connections, protection for PINs and one-time codes, role-based access, permission checks, logical separation of documents and workflows, security and audit logs, and expiry and deletion flows. Access to data is limited by work task and system need.

We minimise personal data in technical logs and audit records: the aim is to store mainly technical metadata, such as event type, user or resource technical identifier, status, time, file type, or size. Application logs are not intended to store document content, conversation content, voice-data buffers, request body previews, raw payment-provider payloads, or authentication or invite token values.

No web service is entirely risk-free. Server backups, systemd/journald logs, proxy logs, and external service-provider technical logs depend on separate operations and provider configurations. For that reason, we recommend entering only the data needed for the workflow at hand.

section11.heading: 11. Changes
section11.body: This Privacy Policy may be updated if platform features, data flows, or legal requirements change. Significant changes will be communicated in the product or by email.

privacy_filter.heading: 4.10 Privacy pre-check and Privacy Filter
privacy_filter.body:
In sensitive workflows, the platform may use a privacy pre-check to detect and mask possible personal data before AI processing, RAG indexing, document drafting, logging, or saving a public help listing. The check may rely on SotsiaalAI's local pattern-based filter and, where deployed, the locally running OpenAI Privacy Filter model.

If possible personal data is detected in the user input, the system may offer choices: the user can edit the text, the system can send a masked version, or in a private professional workflow the original text can be sent after the user's explicit choice. Public help requests and help offers use stricter data minimisation, and continuing with the original sensitive text may be restricted.

The privacy pre-check does not guarantee that every personal-data item or sensitive detail will always be detected. It is an additional safeguard that helps reduce accidental excessive processing; the user and, where relevant, the organisation must still assess the legal basis, necessity of the data, and appropriate workflow.

section1.heading: 1. General Provisions
section1.body:
These Terms of Use govern the use of the SotsiaalAI platform operated by SotsiaalAI OÜ. By using the platform, you confirm that you have read and accepted these Terms.

To keep the service stable, secure, and reasonably usable, the platform may apply technical limits and usage rules. These may cover chat volume, deep research usage, repeated document refinement, and limits on file count, file size, and storage. The applicable limits may depend on the feature or package and are described in these Terms or shown in the product UI where needed.

If you do not agree with these Terms, you must not use the platform. The Privacy Policy and these Terms together form the basis for using the service.

section2.heading: 2. Service Description
section2.body:
SotsiaalAI is a role-based web platform where a user can ask questions in chat, continue in guidance mode, describe help requests and help offers, use document workflows, manage documents, open rooms, send invites, and use profile, subscription, and accessibility settings.

The platform combines AI, retrieval-based knowledge access, and user-selected workflows to support both social work professionals and people seeking help. It is not an emergency channel and does not replace binding official decisions, professional advice, or urgent assistance.

section3.heading: 3. Account and Access
section3.items:
• You must create an account and choose an appropriate role to use the core platform features.
  • You must keep your email, PIN, and other sign-in information confidential and are responsible for actions taken through your account.
  • Sign-in may require an extra verification code sent by email, and you may choose to trust a device for future sign-ins.
  • Many workflows require an active subscription or valid sponsored access.
  • Administrator preview mode or other administrative rights do not grant the right to impersonate another user, view that user's personal conversations or documents, or modify that user's data without a clear legal or technical justification.

section4.heading: 4. Permitted Use
section4.items:
• You must use the platform in compliance with law, good practice, and these Terms.
  • Unlawful, threatening, deceptive, defamatory, hateful, or rights-infringing content is prohibited, including help listings or invites that mislead other users.
  • It is prohibited to bypass security controls, send automated mass requests, exceed or attempt to evade feature-specific usage limits, storage limits, or subscription limits, spread malware, or disrupt the service.
  • It is prohibited to use another person's account without permission, create a false identity, or use the platform in a way that harms other users or SotsiaalAI.
  • It is prohibited to systematically copy platform data or outputs, or use them to build a competing service, without SotsiaalAI's permission.

section5.heading: 5. Subscription, Payments and Cancellation
section5.paragraph1: Paid platform features are provided through a role-based monthly subscription. The monthly fee is 7.99 EUR for a person seeking help and 14.99 EUR for a social work professional, unless a different price is shown on the platform at the time of checkout. Payments are processed by Maksekeskus AS.
section5.paragraph2: Access may be self-paid or sponsored by the person who invited you. Under the current product rules, sponsored access lasts for one month and does not automatically convert into the invitee's own paid subscription. If the invited user already has an active subscription, sponsored checkout may be blocked.
section5.paragraph3: You can manage your subscription on the Subscription page, where you see the active plan, sponsored access end date, and cancellation option, and on the Profile page, where you can open the subscription overview and change account settings. After payment is started, the user is redirected to the Maksekeskus checkout page, and access is activated immediately after payment-provider confirmation. The payment methods shown in checkout are the methods available for that transaction. When cancelled, the next automatic billing cycle stops, but the already paid period remains active until its end unless the law requires otherwise. After the service has been activated and used, refunds are generally not provided unless required by law. SotsiaalAI may change pricing or package terms with reasonable advance notice.

section6.heading: 6. Availability and Maintenance
section6.body:
SotsiaalAI makes reasonable efforts to keep the service available, but uninterrupted operation is not guaranteed. Maintenance, updates, security fixes, failures of external providers, or usage limits may temporarily restrict the platform.

At present, the service applies the following technical limits: up to 50 user turns in one chat; deep research limited to one active job per user and a daily usage limit; the same document may be refined or corrected up to 3 times in a row; and materials may be uploaded up to 10 files at a time and up to 100 MB per day.

SotsiaalAI may apply technical limits, reasonable-use controls, and abuse-prevention measures to protect service operation, security, and fair access. If usage is unusually heavy, repetitive, or appears to bypass limits, SotsiaalAI may temporarily slow, restrict, pause, or reconfigure affected features even if the account itself remains active.

section7.heading: 7. Platform Workflows and Results
section7.body:
SotsiaalAI supports users through the platform's chat, document, help, and room workflows as described on the platform.

In situations involving crisis, violence, child safety, or self-harm risk, the platform may display urgent-help guidance and route the user toward an Estonian support channel or a competent person. This routing does not replace emergency services, child protection, victim support, or professional help.

section8.heading: 8. Professional Use and Processing Framework
section8.body:
SotsiaalAI may be used as a private individual and also as a work tool in workflows where no identifiable personal data of a client or other data subject is entered.

If a user wishes to use SotsiaalAI on behalf of an employer or organisation with identifiable personal data of a client or other data subject, the user must have clear permission from the employer or organisation and, where necessary, a separate contractual data processing framework.

An organisation or employer administrator role does not by itself provide general access to an employee's personal conversations, documents, or files. Room and group conversation content is visible to room members, and data shared by a user is handled according to the visibility rules of the relevant workflow.

Internal confirmations, instructions, or usage permissions between employer and employee do not by themselves create a processor relationship between SotsiaalAI and the organisation. If SotsiaalAI processes personal data on behalf of an organisation, there must also be a written or electronic contractual framework bindingly accepted by SotsiaalAI.

SotsiaalAI may make available on the platform a standard framework document for professional use and data processing or similar terms. Reading such a document, downloading it, or confirming it at employee level does not automatically make it binding on the organisation. If the document is intended to serve as a framework for processing data on behalf of an organisation, it becomes binding on the organisation only after proper signing or other provable electronic acceptance.

For professional use, the organisation should define roles, permissions, allowed data categories, data minimisation rules, review and confirmation procedures, and audit-trail needs before sensitive workflows are used. More sensitive capabilities may be limited to specialists, service providers, municipalities, or organisation-linked users.

section9.heading: 9. Use of AI
section9.paragraph1:
SotsiaalAI uses AI, retrieval-backed knowledge access, and other technical services to answer questions, provide guidance, help draft documents, and support help-request and help-offer workflows. The platform may ask the user to confirm in chat which work mode the current conversation should continue in.

The AI may propose the most likely work mode, answer, classification, match, or draft, but the final decision to continue the workflow and use the output remains with the user. The user understands that AI output may be inaccurate, incomplete, or require correction.
section9.paragraph2:
AI output must not be treated as automatically binding truth, an official position, a professional decision, or the sole basis for a decision.

The platform informs the user that they are interacting with AI functions unless this is already clear from the nature of the workflow or the user interface.

section10.heading: 10. Data Processing in Chats and Uploaded Materials
section10.paragraph1: When using the platform, a user may enter text, send room messages, save help data, invite other people, upload documents, or use voice features. That data is processed to operate the service in accordance with the Privacy Policy.
section10.paragraph2: More detailed rules on data processing, retention periods, deletion-job tracking, logging minimisation, and user rights are described in the Privacy Policy.
section10.items:
• Chat and room content. Conversations, rooms, and their related workflow state are stored to operate the platform, show history, and continue the features chosen by the user. A user's personal conversations are linked to that user's account, and room content is visible to room members.
  • Help listings and invites. The user is responsible for sharing only the data needed to connect with another person or mediate help.
  • Temporary chat files. Files added in the chat document workflow or analysis flow are intended for that session or workflow and are not automatically added to the persistent Documents library.
  • Documents library and agent outputs. If the user uploads a file into the persistent Documents library or approves an agent result, it is generally stored for up to 90 days from the last change, unless the law or a technical link requires a different retention period for a short time. When a document is deleted, it is removed from active document management and deletion of the related file and RAG or search-index reference is triggered where such a reference exists.
  • Account deletion and technical records. When an account is deleted, the user loses access to related active data and the account is removed according to the application's deletion logic. Limited technical audit records and deletion-job records may remain for security, proof of deletion, handling pending deletion jobs, or legal obligations.
  • Dictation and read-aloud. Voice features are used only to provide that feature. The user must ensure they have the right to transmit another person's data through a microphone or file.
  • Sensitive data. If the user enters sensitive information or data about third parties, the user is responsible for having a lawful basis and for sharing only the minimum necessary information.
  • Logging and audit. The platform may store technical logs, audit records, and deletion-job records for service security, reliability, and legal obligations. Application logs are not intended to store document content, conversation content, voice-data buffers, request body previews, raw payment-provider payloads, or authentication or invite token values.
  • Professional-use confirmations. If the user or an organisation representative accepts a professional-use or data processing framework, the platform may store proof records related to that confirmation, including the time, version, role, and other technical details needed for evidence.

section11.heading: 11. Intellectual Property
section11.body:
The platform software, design, data structures, trademarks, and other content created by SotsiaalAI belong to SotsiaalAI or its partners unless stated otherwise. The user retains rights to their own submitted data to the extent provided by law and by their rights in that data.

The user may use outputs created through the platform for lawful purposes in their work, communication, and documentation. Without SotsiaalAI's written permission, you may not systematically copy, distribute, repackage, or reuse the platform's technical solution, structure, service components, or systematic collections of outputs to create a competing service.

section12.heading: 12. Termination of the Agreement
section12.body:
The user may stop using the platform at any time, including by deleting the account or cancelling the subscription. Account deletion may result in loss of access to related documents, conversations, drafts, and other workspace content in accordance with the retention and deletion rules described in the Privacy Policy. Technical audit records, deletion-job records, backups, and system, proxy, or service-provider logs may remain for a limited period to the extent described in the Privacy Policy.

SotsiaalAI may suspend or terminate access if the user materially breaches the Terms, abuses the service, repeatedly exceeds or attempts to bypass feature limits, threatens the safety of other users, or harms the operation of the platform. SotsiaalAI may also temporarily limit specific features to protect service stability, security, or reasonable use.

section13.heading: 13. Changes to the Terms
section13.body:
SotsiaalAI may update these Terms if the service, pricing, workflows, or legal requirements change. A new version will be published on the platform, and material changes will be communicated with reasonable prior notice.

Continuing to use the platform after the changes take effect means you accept the updated Terms unless applicable law requires a different process.

section14.heading: 14. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
section14.body:
These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Estonia.

The parties will first try to resolve disputes by negotiation. If no agreement is reached, the dispute will be resolved by the courts of the Republic of Estonia or through another competent dispute resolution process under applicable law.

Nothing in these Terms limits mandatory rights granted to the user under applicable law.

privacy_filter.heading: 10.1 Privacy pre-check and masking
privacy_filter.body:
Before AI processing, RAG search, document drafting, logging, or saving a public help listing, SotsiaalAI may check whether the input contains personal data or other sensitive information. The check may use the platform's own local rules and, where deployed on the server, the locally running OpenAI Privacy Filter model.

If the check finds a name, personal identification code, address, phone number, email address, or another identifiable detail, the platform may show a warning and offer choices: edit the text, send a masked version, or continue with the original text in an allowed private workflow. Continuing with the original sensitive text may not be available for public help requests or help offers.

Masking is a data-minimisation aid, not full anonymisation or a legal guarantee. The user remains responsible for ensuring that the information entered is necessary for the workflow and that there is an appropriate basis for processing it.

view_mode.intro_worker: Keep case materials, templates, and other files here so the agent can prepare specialist-facing drafts.
view_mode.intro_worker_details:
What this is for
This page is meant to make document drafting faster and more organized. Instead of starting from scratch every time, you can:
- upload the materials you need
- keep reusable templates ready
- choose which files the document drafting agent is allowed to use
- open the drafting view based on those files
- review and download finished drafts or final versions

Add documents
Upload the files you need for work here, such as guidance, client-related materials, sample documents, or ready-made templates.

Set the document role
Each document can be marked, for example, as:
- a template
- background material
- another file type
This helps the system understand how the file should be used.

Allow the agent to use the document
For each file, you can decide whether the agent may use it during document drafting. This gives the specialist control over what information becomes input.

Choose documents for drafting
Once the needed files are selected, you can send them forward into the drafting view. There the agent can use them to create, for example, a draft, summary, letter, or another work text.

Manage finished results
In the lower part of the page you can see outputs that have already been created. You can:
- open them
- copy them
- download them
- delete them when needed

intro.paragraph1: SotsiaalAI is a next-generation support platform for the social sector that provides trustworthy information, AI support, practical tools, and communication options in one digital environment. The platform is intended for people seeking clarity in life-related questions, social-sector professionals, and service providers.
intro.paragraph2: The social sector is characterised by heavy workloads, fragmented information, and difficult navigation across the system of services, benefits, and contacts - as also noted by the OSKA report (2025). SotsiaalAI aims to make social-sector knowledge more accessible and understandable, simplify workflows, and help people gain a clearer understanding of their situation. To do this, the platform brings together social-sector sources, guidance, legal frameworks, methodologies, and practical materials.
intro.paragraph3: The central place to use the platform is the chat window, where users can describe a situation, ask about services, benefits, rights, and professional practice, and clarify possible next steps. AI helps surface relevant options, explain connections, and prepare useful texts and next steps. Sources are shown with answers so the information can be checked. From the same page, users can also start separate workflows, such as help requests, help offers, deep research, pre-inquiries, and document analysis.
intro.paragraph4: SotsiaalAI is built on the principle that AI does not replace a specialist or make the final decision for a person. AI helps people reach knowledge, rights, services, and work tools faster, but sensitive data, crisis situations, child and youth safety, and professional decisions require human review, clear role boundaries, and, when needed, routing to real support channels.
intro.paragraph5: Chat rooms are separate communication spaces where a person seeking help, a social work professional, a service provider, or a colleague can be invited. A room may be created, for example, after matching a request for help with an offer of help or after sharing a request, so that the parties can continue communication and clarify next steps.
intro.paragraph6: The service map is SotsiaalAI's map-based application that helps users find social-sector points of contact and contact details. At first, the service map focuses on local-government social welfare contacts and service providers so that the necessary information and situation-related options are more visible to the user.
intro.paragraph7: Our vision is to become a digital environment for the social sector that connects trustworthy information, practical tools, service providers, and communication. SotsiaalAI supports people in different life and work situations - whether the question is related to finding a service, benefit, document, request for help or offer of help, career counselling, job opportunity, or assistive aid.
intro.paragraph8: SotsiaalAI is being developed as a social enterprise and a social innovation initiative. We combine technology and social-sector practice to reduce information barriers and make workflows, collaboration, and finding help clearer, faster, and more accessible. In developing the platform, we consider safe use, data protection, and clear usage rules important.

author.title: From one idea to a real platform
author.byline: Laur Raudsoo, owner and management board member
paragraphs: SotsiaalAI lugu sai alguse ligi kümme aastat tagasi, 2017. aastal, kui tekkis mõte luua portaal nimega sotsiaalinfo.ee. Tol hetkel ei olnud mul veel pikka tööstaaži sotsiaalvaldkonnas, kuid praktiline side valdkonnaga oli juba kujunenud õpingute, töö, praktika ja erialase vabatahtliku tegevuse kaudu. Olin äsja lõpetanud Tallinna Ülikoolis sotsiaaltöö bakalaureuseõppe, tegutsenud Eesti Sotsiaaltöö Assotsiatsioonis, töötanud isikliku abistajana, olnud seotud MTÜ Helpific platvormiarenduse ja rahvusvahelise koostööga ning läbinud Sotsiaalministeeriumis praktika, mille fookuses oli puuetega inimeste toetuste ja hüvitiste analüüs. Samal aastal alustasin tööd ajakirja Sotsiaaltöö tegevtoimetajana.

Nii kasvas idee välja minu kokkupuutest sotsiaalvaldkonna, ligipääsetavuse, digilahenduste ja sotsiaalse ettevõtlusega. Juba siis oli lähtekoht selge: sama keskkond ei saa kõnetada kõiki ühtemoodi. Sotsiaalvaldkonna spetsialist vajab tööks sügavamat teadmusruumi ja professionaalseid töövahendeid, eluküsimusega pöörduja aga selget, arusaadavat ja ligipääsetavat tuge.

Nii tekkis varakult arusaam lahendusest, mis ühendab mõlemad pooled, kuid arvestab kummagi vajadustega. Ühele poole pidi koonduma spetsialistile vajalik erialane sisu praktikate, ajakirja Sotsiaaltöö, uuringute, juhendmaterjalide, seadusandluse, kohtulahendite, sündmuste ja koostööpartnerite infoga. Teisele poole kujunes mõte keskkonnast, kus keskmes on inimesele arusaadav ja praktiline abi.

Järgmine oluline samm tuli 2020. aastal, kui idee liikus edasi sotsiaalabi mobiilirakenduse suunas. Keskseks muutus küsimus, kuidas inimene jõuab teenuste, toetuste ja abi taotlemiseni lihtsamal, arusaadavamal ja praktilisemal viisil. Just selles etapis tekkis ka mõte rakendada platvormil tehisintellekti - ligikaudu kaks aastat enne ChatGPT jõudmist avalikkuse ette.

Mõne aasta pärast, 2022. aastaks, oli visioon muutunud märksa selgemaks ja terviklikumaks. Ühelt poolt joonistus välja sotsiaaltöötajatele mõeldud platvormi mõte, mis toetaks kovisiooni, anonüümsete juhtumite arutamist ja professionaalset koostööd. Teisalt püsis sama olulisena suund, kus eluküsimusega pöördujal oleks lihtsam jõuda vajaliku info, teenuste ja järgmiste sammudeni.

Samal ajal muutus konkreetsemaks ka tehisintellekti roll: AI-d nähti abilisena, mis võiks vähendada töökoormust, analüüsida andmeid, toetada ennetust, aidata juhtumite lahendamisel ja pakkuda eluküsimusega pöördujale selgemat tuge. Nii hakkas kujunema terviklikum digilahenduse visioon, milles spetsialisti töövood, eluküsimusega pöörduja kasutustee, andmed ja tehisintellekti võimalused moodustavad omavahel seotud terviku.

Reaalne arendustöö algas 2025. aasta mais. Selleks ajaks oli varasemast portaali- ja mobiilirakenduse ideest kujunenud üks lahendus, mida saab kasutada nii arvutis kui ka mobiiliseadmes ning paigaldada ka rakendusena. Arendustöö eesmärk oli viia aastatega kujunenud visioon lahenduseks, kus nii spetsialistile kui ka eluküsimusega pöördujale on kujundatud oma loogika, materjalid ja töövood. Selle kõrval algas ka platvormi teadmiste ja materjalide koondamine, millele tehisintellekti assistendid saavad vastamisel toetuda.

Peaaegu aasta hiljem, 2026. aasta aprilliks, oli algsest ideest kujunenud tehisintellektil põhinev abiplatvorm SotsiaalAI. Platvormil on kaks tehisintellekti assistenti: üks sotsiaalvaldkonna spetsialistile ja teine eluküsimusega pöördujale. SotsiaalAI keskmes on vestluspõhine kasutusloogika, mis aitab leida infot, saada juhiseid ja liikuda edasi järgmiste sammudeni. Platvormis saab liikuda eri režiimide, agentide ja töövoogude vahel, näiteks koostada dokumente ja tekste, hallata faile, analüüsida materjale ning teha süvauuringut. Spetsialistidele pakub platvorm ka ühiseid vestlusruume koostööks, kovisiooniks ja vajadusel kliendiga suhtlemiseks.
Samuti saab platvormil kirjeldada nii vabatahtlikke kui ka tasulisi abisoove ja abipakkumisi ning vormistada need tehisaru abil kuulutusteks. See võimalus on kõigile platvormi kasutajatele tasuta.

Ligipääsetavuse toetamiseks saab kasutaja kohandada platvormi keelt, kontrasti, teksti suurust, teemat, ekraani profiili ning liikumise ja jõudluse seadeid. Vestluses saab kasutada ka dikteerimist ja vastuste ettelugemist, mis muudab platvormi kasutamise paindlikumaks eri vajaduste, seadmete ja kasutusolukordade korral.

SotsiaalAI's vision is to become a next-generation support platform for the social sector, centred around a single chat window. SotsiaalAI brings together social-sector knowledge, AI support, practical tools, and matching requests for help with offers of help. Its direction of development is to move toward a comprehensive everyday coping environment that can also help people find support with debt counselling, career counselling, work and housing opportunities, and questions related to assistive aids.

SotsiaalAI lugu on ligi kümne aasta pikkune teekond esimesest ideest toimiva lahenduseni. Selles peegelduvad nii sotsiaalne ettevõtlus kui ka innovatsioon: platvormi arendatakse teadlikult ja hoolikalt, et SotsiaalAI-st kujuneks sisuliselt väärtuslik, hästi toimiv ja turvaline platvorm, mis võiks jõuda iga eestlase taskusse.

features_page.title: SotsiaalAI features
features_page.intro: This page summarises SotsiaalAI's core functions: what each feature does, who it is for, and which privacy, safety, and human-review principles apply in the workflows.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.body: The workspace is a role-based overview and work area that shows the tools suited to the user's role. The help-seeker view focuses on help requests and offers, pre-inquiries, document drafting, invites, and the service map. The social work professional view adds documents, inquiry intake, co-vision, and materials. The service provider view adds the service profile, pre-inquiry intake, and visibility related to the service map. Some tools may require active access or a subscription.

help.title: Help requests and help offers
help.body: A help request or help offer can be described in the user's own words. SotsiaalAI helps make it clearer, asks follow-up questions when needed, and helps make the completed listing visible. The platform tries to find suitable help requests and help offers, and when there is a match, the parties can continue communication in a shared chat room.

service_card.title: Service card
service_card.body: The service card is SotsiaalAI's map-based application for finding key social-sector contacts and places to turn to. The initial focus is on local-government social-welfare contacts and service providers visible in SotsiaalAI. The map helps users reach a specific contact or service provider faster, without having to search across separate websites.

knowledge_base.title: SotsiaalAI knowledge base
knowledge_base.body: The SotsiaalAI knowledge base is a background layer that supports assistant answers with domain information: municipal service and contact information, application forms, legal acts, guidance, methodology materials, organisation and service-provider information, practical materials, and field articles or studies. It helps keep answers in a social-sector context and reduces the need to search for the same information manually across separate places.

assistants_agents.title: SotsiaalAI assistants and agents
assistants_agents.body: SotsiaalAI assistants and agents support users as they work with a question, document, or situation description. The conversation supports asking for information, making sense of a situation, and user-selected workflows such as document drafting, document analysis, deep research, a pre-inquiry, a help request, or a help offer. The user decides which tool to use. The assistant can help organize information, clarify questions, and prepare content when needed.

sources.title: Source display
sources.body: Source display helps users see what an answer is based on. It supports checking the answer and distinguishing whether information comes from legislation, guidance, a local-government website, or another used material. In social-sector questions this is especially important because information needs to be reliable and up to date.

drafting.title: Document drafting
drafting.body: SotsiaalAI helps draft letters, applications, reports, and other needed documents. The user can describe what they want to prepare, and the platform helps structure, expand, and refine the text. Document drafting can use existing materials, templates, conversation content, or knowledge-base information when needed.

analysis.title: Document analysis
analysis.body: Document analysis allows the user to upload a file and ask for a summary, explanations, or important observations. It is useful for understanding an official response, guide, contract, report, decision, or another document. When needed, the analysis can also take the SotsiaalAI knowledge base into account to connect the document content with a wider field context.

research.title: Deep research
research.body: Deep research is useful when the user needs a more thorough overview of a social-sector topic, service, law, guide, or practical question. SotsiaalAI gathers the information into a structured answer or report, highlighting the main observations, connections, and sources.

rooms.title: Chat rooms
rooms.body: Chat rooms can be used for shared conversations and to invite, for example, a person seeking help, a social work professional, a service provider, or a colleague. A room may also be created through a match between a help request and a help offer, or when information needs to be clarified with another party after a pre-inquiry. If needed, a person can be invited to a chat room and given one month of full platform access paid by the inviter.

documents.title: Documents: storage and management
documents.body: The documents view gathers uploaded files, created texts, templates, and working versions in one place. This makes it possible to reopen, refine, and, when needed, use documents later in new conversations, document drafting, or other workflows.

pre_inquiry.title: Pre-inquiry
pre_inquiry.body: A pre-inquiry is a workflow for pre-assessment and preparing an inquiry. The assistant helps clarify the situation, notice risk and personal-data signals, suggest suitable municipal contacts or service providers from the service-map data layer, and prepare an editable inquiry draft. The draft can be saved, copied, downloaded, or opened as an email; when internal SotsiaalAI intake is available, the recipient can open the inquiry in the platform and continue in a chat room if needed. A pre-inquiry does not replace an official assessment of need or decision-making.

intake.title: Inquiry intake
intake.body: Inquiry intake allows a social work professional or service provider to receive pre-inquiries or requests sent through the platform with the user's consent. It helps start communication with better background information and, when needed, move on to a chat room, clarifications, next actions, or a meeting.

kovisioon.title: Co-vision and practice examples
kovisioon.body: Co-vision is a closed peer discussion of a case between professionals. The professional describes the situation themselves and invites colleagues into the discussion. The AI assistant has a supporting role: it helps structure the discussion, notice identifiable details, formulate questions, and prepare a summary. After the discussion, a generalised example case or practice experience can be created for other professionals if desired.

materials_adding.title: Adding materials
materials_adding.body: Professionals and service providers can send documents, guides, service descriptions, and other sources to supplement the SotsiaalAI knowledge base. Materials are reviewed before being added to the knowledge base, so the platform content stays relevant and reliable.

service_card_listing.title: Service provider card profile
service_card_listing.body: The service provider card profile makes the service provider visible on the service card. The card can show the provider's location or service area, main services, target groups, and contact details, so the user can find the right place to turn to faster.

service_profile.title: Service profile and management
service_profile.body: The service profile helps a service provider be findable in SotsiaalAI both on the map and in assistant conversations. The service provider can describe their services, target groups, region, contacts, languages, and accessibility information. This lets the platform use current information on the service card, in the knowledge base, and when answering user questions.

privacy_safety.title: Privacy pre-check
privacy_safety.body: For sensitive text, SotsiaalAI supports data minimisation: in pre-inquiries and work drafts, simple personal-data signals are detected and the user is warned before continuing with AI processing, saving, or sharing. The goal is to enter only the information the workflow actually needs.

crisis_routing.title: Crisis and risk routing
crisis_routing.body: When a description points to immediate danger, suicide risk, violence, or another serious crisis, AI does not remain the only channel. The platform surfaces Estonian support channels such as 112, Child Helpline 116 111, and Victim Support 116 006, and routes the user toward a person or competent service.

youth_child_safety.title: Youth and child safety
youth_child_safety.body: Signals related to minors, child protection, school bullying, domestic violence, abuse, or neglect are treated as a higher-risk workflow. AI may help structure the situation, but it does not make the final risk assessment or replace child protection, emergency services, or a specialist decision.

trusted_access_security.title: Role-based access and account security
trusted_access_security.body: More sensitive workflows are not the same for everyone. Help seekers, specialists, service providers, municipalities, and administrators have different visibility and permissions; professional use requires clearer roles, audit trails, and the principle that an admin does not see users' private conversations by default.

workflow_orchestration.title: Multi-step workflows
workflow_orchestration.body: Pre-inquiries, document drafting, document analysis, and deep research are not just one chat answer. They move through steps: mapping the situation, checking risks and sources, preparing a draft, human review, and confirmation.

domain_knowledge.title: Social-sector knowledge environment
domain_knowledge.body: The value of SotsiaalAI is not a generic chatbot, but a knowledge base and workflow logic built for the social sector. The platform uses information grounded in social welfare law, child protection law, municipal services, national guidance, and domain materials where sources are available.

guide.title: How to use the SotsiaalAI platform?
guide.intro: This guide covers creating an account, activating access, using chat, settings, help and document work, group conversations, and profile features in SotsiaalAI.

accessibility.title: 1. Language and accessibility
accessibility.body:
On your first visit the Language and accessibility window opens automatically. You can reopen the same view later on the Profile page.

  • Language. Choose Estonian, Russian, or English.
  • Screen profile. Choose "Mobile / laptop screen", "Apple laptop", or "External monitor / PC" so the display fits your device better.
  • Text size. Adjust the text size to what feels most comfortable.
  • Contrast. Enable stronger contrast when needed.
  • Motion and performance. This section now has two separate options: "Reduce animations" and "Reduce transparency".
  • Performance on older devices. Reducing transparency and animations can improve performance on older devices.
  • Theme. You can switch the color mode between light, dim, dark, and night themes.
  • Save and reset. Changes apply immediately, but you can change preferences on the Profile page and save them again when needed.

home.title: 2. Home page
home.body:
The home page shows two role cards: "For social work professionals" and "For people seeking help".

  • If you are not signed in, clicking a role card opens the sign-in flow, where you can also move to registration.
  • If you are already signed in, clicking a role card opens the chat view for that role.
  • The ABOUT panel sits below the role cards and includes platform information and quick links.
  • From the ABOUT panel you can open the guide, terms of use, privacy policy, Features, Pricing, and contact view. Features explains the platform's core functions; Pricing shows the price list.

register.title: 3. Registration and access activation
register.body:
You need an account to use SotsiaalAI. To actively use chat, the account also needs an active subscription or sponsored access.

3.1 Registration

  1. Choose the relevant role card on the home page and open Register.
  2. Fill in the form with your email address and PIN code (4-8 digits).
  3. Accept the terms of use and privacy policy, and confirm that you have read the platform guide.
  4. If you choose the role Social work professional and want to use the platform on behalf of an employer or organisation with identifiable data about a client or another person, additional professional-use and data-processing conditions may apply.
  5. After registration, we send you a verification email. To activate your account, open the message and confirm your email address.

3.2 Subscription or sponsored access

  • After verification you can sign in with your email and PIN.
  • If you do not have active access, the platform directs you to the Subscription page.
  • When you start the subscription, you are redirected to the Maksekeskus checkout page. Access is activated after payment-provider confirmation.
  • Monthly pricing is role-based: 7.99 EUR per month for a person seeking help and 14.99 EUR per month for a social work professional.
  • If your access comes from a sponsored invite, it stays active for the sponsored period, usually one month, without a separate payment. After that, you can activate your own subscription to continue.

signin.title: 4. Sign-in and role-based navigation
signin.body:
Sign-in starts by choosing the relevant role card on the home page.

  • Enter your email address and PIN.
  • On a previously trusted device, the PIN is usually enough.
  • If you sign in from a new or unusual environment, we send a 6-digit verification code to your email.
  • You can optionally select "Remember this device for 30 days".
  • If you forgot your PIN, use Forgot PIN.
  • After a successful sign-in, the chat view for your role opens. If access is limited, the platform shows the next steps.

chat.title: 5. Chat page
chat.body:
The chat page opens after sign-in. It has an automatic assistant mode where you can ask for information, explanations, and next steps. The page is split into three areas: the left icon rail, the main chat area, and the right icon rail.

Left icon rail

  • Back. Return to the home page.
  • New conversation. Quick action for starting a new conversation.
  • Conversations. Open previous conversations.

Right icon rail

  • Profile. Open account, subscription, and accessibility settings.
  • Rooms. Open existing group conversations.
  • Workspace. Open the role-based workspace. A person seeking help, service provider, and social work professional each see the tools and management views intended for their role.

Workspace

  • The workspace gives access to role-appropriate functions such as help requests, help offers, pre-inquiries, documents, document drafting, covision, invites, materials, service map, and service profile.
  • Short descriptions of these functions are available from the home page ABOUT panel under Features.

Main chat area

  • Type your message in the input field. The input row has a dictation button and, to its right, the send button.
  • While the assistant is responding, the send button becomes a stop button.
  • Use the dictation button to turn speech into text.
  • Actions related to an assistant message appear under that message: sources, read aloud, and copy, when they are available for that reply.
  • When you move to earlier messages, an arrow appears that takes you quickly back to the end of the conversation.
  • If the conversation indicates a possible crisis, the platform shows a red urgent-help notice with guidance.

Workflows and modes

  • Plus button (+). The + next to the message field opens quick options: Help request, Help offer, Deep research, and Document analysis.
  • Help request and Help offer. Use these options to describe a need for help or an offer of help. The assistant helps turn it into a clear listing, asks for missing details when needed, and shows matches after confirmation.
  • Deep research. Starts a more detailed research mode. To exit the mode, press the magnifying-glass icon.
  • Document analysis. Opens the file and analysis panel where you can ask for explanations about a document.
  • Documents, document drafting, covision, materials, service map, service profile, and other role-based tools open through the Workspace when they are available for your role.

documents.title: 6. Documents
documents.body:
The Documents page is meant for a social work professional. It is the place for managing persistent work files and results created in chat or document drafting.

  • You can upload documents, open their detail views, and download files.
  • Documents can be marked for use in a workflow so they are included in agent work or other document tasks.
  • The page also shows drafts and approved results created from those documents.
  • Results can be opened in detail view, saved, approved, and after approval downloaded as DOCX or PDF files.
  • If a chat-created draft is meant for the specialist's work, it appears on this page.

agent_mode.title: 7. Document drafting
agent_mode.body:
The Document drafting page is a workspace where the agent helps create texts, reports, and drafts from selected files. For a person seeking help, this is the main place to continue documents that started in chat.

  • You can choose the task type, tone, language, length, and, when needed, a template.
  • A help seeker can add files needed for their work directly in Document drafting and use the role-based file limit there.
  • The conversation with the agent happens on the same page: you can give the first instruction, ask for refinements, and generate new versions.
  • The page shows the current draft, version history, and previously saved or finished results.
  • If a chat-created result is meant for the help seeker's work, it opens here for further editing.

profile.title: 8. Profile
profile.body:
The Profile page gathers your account, subscription, and personal preferences.

  • The main actions open from the orbital menu in the center of the page.
  • Update PIN. Opens a separate page where you enter the current PIN, the new PIN, and confirmation.
  • Update email. Opens a separate page where you enter the new email address and confirm the change with your current PIN.
  • Manage subscription. Opens the subscription page, where you can see the active plan, sponsored-access end date, and role-based monthly pricing: 7.99 EUR per month for a person seeking help and 14.99 EUR per month for a social work professional.
  • Language and accessibility. Open language, screen profile, text size, contrast, and the Motion and performance settings; changes apply immediately.
  • Theme. Switch between light, dim, dark, and night color modes.
  • Account settings. A page where you can sign out on this device, sign out on all devices, or start account deletion.

about.title: 9. ABOUT
about.body:
The ABOUT section is below the role cards on the home page. It includes a short explanation of the platform and quick links to the guide, terms of use, privacy policy, Features, Pricing, and contact view.

Features describes the main SotsiaalAI functions and who they are intended for. Pricing shows the price list and package comparison.

before_use.title: 10. Before using
before_use.body:
The most important links are in the home page ABOUT panel.

  • User guide. Opens this guide page.
  • Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. Open the platform's terms and data-use pages.
  • Features. Describes the workspace, knowledge base, assistants, help requests, help offers, deep research, documents, pre-inquiries, covision, materials, service map, and service profile.
  • Pricing. Opens the price list and package comparison.
  • Contact. Opens the SotsiaalAI contact view.

quickstart.title: 12. Quick overview
quickstart.body:
If you want to get started quickly, follow these steps:

  1. Choose your role on the home page and create an account.
  2. Accept the policies, confirm that you have read the platform guide, and then verify your email.
  3. Activate a subscription. Monthly pricing is 7.99 EUR for a person seeking help and 14.99 EUR for a social work professional.
  4. Sign in and open the chat page.
  5. Write your question or describe your situation.
  6. Use the plus button for quick options: help request, help offer, deep research, or document analysis.
  7. Use the left icon rail for conversations and the right icon rail for Profile, Rooms, and Workspace. The Workspace contains the tools available for your role.
  8. Function descriptions are in the home page ABOUT panel under Features, and the price list is on Pricing.
  9. Manage settings, account, and subscription on the Profile page.

privacy_safety.title: 11. Privacy warnings and masking
privacy_safety.body:
If you write personal data into a chat, pre-inquiry, document drafting instruction, or help listing, SotsiaalAI may show a privacy warning before sending. This means the text may contain a name, personal identification code, address, phone number, email address, or another sensitive detail.

  • Edit text keeps the text in the input field and nothing is sent.
  • Send masked replaces sensitive parts with labels such as [address] or [phone number] and sends the masked version.
  • Send anyway may be available only in private or professional workflows. It may not be available in a public help request or help offer.

When drafting documents, the system may also mask source material before sending it to AI or RAG search. This helps reduce excessive data processing, but the final draft must always be reviewed by a person.

guide.title: How to read this
guide.units_value: Normalized internal usage units for comparison and thresholding, not exact provider billing.
guide.approx_eur_value: Approximate EUR is a provider-cost-equivalent management view. It helps product and pricing decisions, but it is still not invoice-grade billing.
guide.breakdowns_value: Route/stage shows where usage happens, role/package shows who carries it, model shows which model family drives it.
guide.top_value: Look for concentration, repeated heavy stages, and sharp differences between direct and estimated usage.
guide.coverage_value: Some usage is direct from provider responses, some is estimated. Treat this as an operational dashboard, not an invoice.
guide.actions_value: Before changing pricing or limits, check coverage, route/stage concentration, user/package threshold status, and whether heavy usage is expected.
